prompt:
A small servo-powered gripper is mounted on a flat base or the end of a short arm, shown grasping a lightweight object such as a sponge cube or a 3D-printed block. The gripper has two opposing jaws made from plastic or 3D-printed parts, driven by a single hobby servo with a visible horn and linkage connecting to the jaws. Wires run from the servo to a nearby microcontroller board, clearly indicating that the claw is actuated and controllable.

The backdrop is a bright solarpunk robotics workbench with soft, diffused lighting, a few tools, and neatly organized components in bins. The image is a lightly stylized but mostly photorealistic 512×512 frame with no brand logos or text, focusing on the gripper mechanism and the object it is holding while the rest of the workspace remains subtly out of focus.
